sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Now I see! There are 17 "w" objects:
- 4 vertical bars at rows 35-37, cols 8, 13, 18, 23 (left panel)
- 1 vertical bar at rows 35-37, col 39 (right panel!)
- 4 horizontal bars at row 39, cols 7-9, 12-14, 17-19, 22-24 (left panel)
- 4 vertical bars at rows 41-43, cols 8, 13, 18, 23 (left panel)
- 4 horizontal bars at row 45, cols 7-9, 12-14, 17-19, 22-24 (left panel)
So clicking the right panel button at (36, 39) created a new "w" object at the same position (rows 35-37, col 39) in the right panel. This means clicking the right panel buttons converts them from "B" (black) to "w" (gray), making them match the left panel buttons.
So the mechanic is: clicking a right panel button converts it from "B" to "w". This is the opposite of what I want for the left panel.
But wait, maybe the goal is NOT to clear the left panel buttons. Maybe the goal is to make the right panel match the left panel, or vice versa. Or maybe the goal is to have all buttons be "w" objects.
Actually, let me reconsider the whole game. In level 1, I cleared the right panel buttons by clicking them. In level 2, the left panel has "w" buttons and the right panel has "B" buttons. Maybe the goal is to convert the right panel buttons to "w" objects to match the left panel.
But that doesn't make sense either, because the right panel buttons are already in a different state (black "B" objects).
Wait, let me think about this differently. The right panel buttons are in the "cleared" state (black "B" objects). When I click them, they become "w" objects (uncleared). This suggests that the right panel buttons are the "template" or "target" state, and the left panel buttons are the "current" state.
But the left panel has "w" objects (uncleared) and the right panel has "B" objects (cleared). So maybe the goal is to clear the left panel buttons to match the right panel.
But I can't clear the left panel buttons directly. The only way to clear them is by clicking the yellow in the gray panel, which only clears the bottom row.
Hmm, let me think about this more. Maybe the right panel buttons are NOT the template. Maybe they're the buttons that I need to click to clear the corresponding left panel buttons.
Wait, but when I clicked the right panel button at (36, 39), it created a "w" object at the same position. This means the right panel button was converted from "B" to "w". This is the opposite of clearing.
Actually, maybe I need to click the left panel buttons to clear them. Let me try clicking a left panel button again, but this time after the right panel button was clicked.
Actually, wait. Let me re-examine the game. The right panel has "B" objects (black) and the left panel has "w" objects (gray). When I click a right panel "B" object, it becomes a "w" object. This suggests that clicking "B" objects converts them to "w" objects.
But the left panel has "w" objects. Maybe clicking "w" objects converts them to "B" objects (clearing them). Let me try clicking a left panel "w" object.
Actually, I already tried clicking left panel buttons earlier and they were cosmetic. But maybe the state was different then. Let me try again now.
Actually, wait. Let me re-read the earlier results. When I clicked the left panel button at (35, 8), the "w" count remained at 16. So clicking left panel buttons was cosmetic.
But now the "w" count is 17 (because I added one by clicking the right panel button). Let me try clicking the left panel button again to see if it clears the corresponding right panel button.
Actually, let me think about this differently. The right panel button at (36, 39) was converted from "B" to "w" when I clicked it. This means the right panel button is now in the same state as the left panel buttons. Maybe the goal is to have all buttons be "w" objects, and then something happens.
But that doesn't make sense either, because the left panel buttons are "w" objects and they're not cleared.
Let me try a different approach. Let me click all the right panel buttons to convert them to "w" objects, and see if that triggers the level completion.
Actually, that would take many actions and I'm running low on the efficiency budget. Let me try a different approach.
Let me undo the last action by clicking the right panel button again. Maybe clicking it again converts it back to "B".
